principles.name: Perceivable
principles.description: Information and user interface components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive.

guidelines.name: Text Alternatives
guidelines.description: Provide text alternatives for any non-text content so that it can be changed into other forms people need.

success_criteria.name: Non-text Content
success_criteria.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.

success_criteria.name: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: For prerecorded audio-only and video-only media, a text alternative or audio track is provided.

success_criteria.name: Captions (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

success_criteria.name: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: An alternative or audio description is provided for prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

success_criteria.name: Captions (Live)
success_criteria.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

success_criteria.name: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

success_criteria.name: Sign Language (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

success_criteria.name: Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: Extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media where pauses are insufficient.

success_criteria.name: Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
success_criteria.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media and video-only media.

success_criteria.name: Audio-only (Live)
success_criteria.description: An alternative is provided for live audio-only content.

guidelines.name: Adaptable
guidelines.description: Create content that can be presented in different ways without losing information or structure.

success_criteria.name: Info and Relationships
success_criteria.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

success_criteria.name: Meaningful Sequence
success_criteria.description: When the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

success_criteria.name: Sensory Characteristics
success_criteria.description: Instructions do not rely solely on sensory characteristics such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

success_criteria.name: Orientation
success_criteria.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation unless essential.

success_criteria.name: Identify Input Purpose
success_criteria.description: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined.

success_criteria.name: Identify Purpose
success_criteria.description: The purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.

guidelines.name: Distinguishable
guidelines.description: Make it easier for users to see and hear content including separating foreground from background.

success_criteria.name: Use of Color
success_criteria.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.

success_criteria.name: Audio Control
success_criteria.description: If audio plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, a mechanism is available to pause, stop, or control volume.

success_criteria.name: Contrast (Minimum)
success_criteria.description: Text and images of text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text).
success_criteria.apca_note: APCA recommends Lc 75 for body text, Lc 60 for large or bold text as a more perceptually accurate alternative.

success_criteria.name: Resize Text
success_criteria.description: Text can be resized up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality.

success_criteria.name: Images of Text
success_criteria.description: Text is used to convey information rather than images of text except where essential.

success_criteria.name: Contrast (Enhanced)
success_criteria.description: Text and images of text have a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 (4.5:1 for large text).

success_criteria.name: Low or No Background Audio
success_criteria.description: Prerecorded audio-only content with primarily speech has no or low background sounds.

success_criteria.name: Visual Presentation
success_criteria.description: A mechanism is available for users to control the visual presentation of blocks of text.

success_criteria.name: Images of Text (No Exception)
success_criteria.description: Images of text are only used for pure decoration or where essential.

success_criteria.name: Reflow
success_criteria.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or two-dimensional scrolling at 320 CSS pixels width.

success_criteria.name: Non-text Contrast
success_criteria.description: User interface components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent colours.

success_criteria.name: Text Spacing
success_criteria.description: No loss of content or functionality occurs when overriding text spacing properties.

success_criteria.name: Content on Hover or Focus
success_criteria.description: Additional content that appears on hover or focus is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent.

principles.name: Operable
principles.description: User interface components and navigation must be operable.

guidelines.name: Keyboard Accessible
guidelines.description: Make all functionality available from a keyboard.

success_criteria.name: Keyboard
success_criteria.description: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings.

success_criteria.name: No Keyboard Trap
success_criteria.description: Keyboard focus can be moved away from any component using only a keyboard interface.

success_criteria.name: Keyboard (No Exception)
success_criteria.description: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for any keystroke.

success_criteria.name: Character Key Shortcuts
success_criteria.description: Keyboard shortcuts using only character keys can be turned off, remapped, or only active on focus.

guidelines.name: Enough Time
guidelines.description: Provide users enough time to read and use content.

success_criteria.name: Timing Adjustable
success_criteria.description: Time limits can be turned off, adjusted, or extended.

success_criteria.name: Pause, Stop, Hide
success_criteria.description: Moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating content can be paused, stopped, or hidden.

success_criteria.name: No Timing
success_criteria.description: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity.

success_criteria.name: Interruptions
success_criteria.description: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user.

success_criteria.name: Re-authenticating
success_criteria.description: Data is preserved when an authenticated session expires.

success_criteria.name: Timeouts
success_criteria.description: Users are warned of the duration of any inactivity that could cause data loss.

guidelines.name: Seizures and Physical Reactions
guidelines.description: Do not design content in a way that is known to cause seizures or physical reactions.

success_criteria.name: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
success_criteria.description: Content does not contain anything that flashes more than three times per second, or the flash is below general flash and red flash thresholds.

success_criteria.name: Three Flashes
success_criteria.description: Content does not contain anything that flashes more than three times per second.

success_criteria.name: Animation from Interactions
success_criteria.description: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled unless essential.

guidelines.name: Navigable
guidelines.description: Provide ways to help users navigate, find content, and determine where they are.

success_criteria.name: Bypass Blocks
success_criteria.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.

success_criteria.name: Page Titled
success_criteria.description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose.

success_criteria.name: Focus Order
success_criteria.description: Focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

success_criteria.name: Link Purpose (In Context)
success_criteria.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from its context.

success_criteria.name: Multiple Ways
success_criteria.description: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages.

success_criteria.name: Headings and Labels
success_criteria.description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

success_criteria.name: Focus Visible
success_criteria.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

success_criteria.name: Location
success_criteria.description: Information about the user's location within a set of web pages is available.

success_criteria.name: Link Purpose (Link Only)
success_criteria.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone.

success_criteria.name: Section Headings
success_criteria.description: Section headings are used to organize the content.

success_criteria.name: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
success_criteria.description: When a component receives keyboard focus, it is not entirely hidden due to author-created content.

success_criteria.name: Focus Not Obscured (Enhanced)
success_criteria.description: When a component receives keyboard focus, no part of it is hidden due to author-created content.

success_criteria.name: Focus Appearance
success_criteria.description: The keyboard focus indicator has sufficient size and contrast to be clearly visible.

guidelines.name: Input Modalities
guidelines.description: Make it easier for users to operate functionality through various inputs beyond keyboard.

success_criteria.name: Pointer Gestures
success_criteria.description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures can be operated with a single pointer.

success_criteria.name: Pointer Cancellation
success_criteria.description: For functionality operated with a single pointer, at least one of: no down-event, abort/undo, up-reversal, or essential.

success_criteria.name: Label in Name
success_criteria.description: For user interface components with a visible text label, the accessible name contains the visible text.

success_criteria.name: Motion Actuation
success_criteria.description: Functionality triggered by device motion can be operated by user interface components and motion response can be disabled.

success_criteria.name: Target Size (Enhanced)
success_criteria.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44x44 CSS pixels.

success_criteria.name: Concurrent Input Mechanisms
success_criteria.description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform.

success_criteria.name: Dragging Movements
success_criteria.description: All functionality that uses dragging movements can be achieved with a single pointer without dragging.

success_criteria.name: Target Size (Minimum)
success_criteria.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24x24 CSS pixels.

principles.name: Understandable
principles.description: Information and the operation of the user interface must be understandable.

guidelines.name: Readable
guidelines.description: Make text content readable and understandable.

success_criteria.name: Language of Page
success_criteria.description: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined.

success_criteria.name: Language of Parts
success_criteria.description: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined.

success_criteria.name: Unusual Words
success_criteria.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words used in an unusual or restricted way.

success_criteria.name: Abbreviations
success_criteria.description: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available.

success_criteria.name: Reading Level
success_criteria.description: When text requires reading ability more advanced than lower secondary education level, supplemental content is available.

success_criteria.name: Pronunciation
success_criteria.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning is ambiguous without pronunciation.

guidelines.name: Predictable
guidelines.description: Make web pages appear and operate in predictable ways.

success_criteria.name: On Focus
success_criteria.description: When any user interface component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context.

success_criteria.name: On Input
success_criteria.description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context.

success_criteria.name: Consistent Navigation
success_criteria.description: Navigation mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages occur in the same relative order.

success_criteria.name: Consistent Identification
success_criteria.description: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages are identified consistently.

success_criteria.name: Change on Request
success_criteria.description: Changes of context are initiated only by user request or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes.

success_criteria.name: Consistent Help
success_criteria.description: If a web page provides help mechanisms, they occur in the same relative order across pages.

guidelines.name: Input Assistance
guidelines.description: Help users avoid and correct mistakes.

success_criteria.name: Error Identification
success_criteria.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text.

success_criteria.name: Labels or Instructions
success_criteria.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

success_criteria.name: Error Suggestion
success_criteria.description: If an input error is detected and suggestions for correction are known, the suggestion is provided to the user.

success_criteria.name: Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
success_criteria.description: For pages causing legal commitments or financial transactions, submissions are reversible, checked, or confirmed.

success_criteria.name: Help
success_criteria.description: Context-sensitive help is available.

success_criteria.name: Error Prevention (All)
success_criteria.description: For pages requiring user submissions, submissions are reversible, checked, or confirmed.

success_criteria.name: Redundant Entry
success_criteria.description: Information previously entered by the user is auto-populated or available for selection in the same process.

success_criteria.name: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
success_criteria.description: Authentication processes do not rely on cognitive function tests unless alternatives are provided.

success_criteria.name: Accessible Authentication (Enhanced)
success_criteria.description: Authentication processes do not rely on cognitive function tests.

principles.name: Robust
principles.description: Content must be robust enough that it can be interpreted by a wide variety of user agents, including assistive technologies.

guidelines.name: Compatible
guidelines.description: Maximise compatibility with current and future user agents, including assistive technologies.

success_criteria.name: Parsing (Obsolete and removed)
success_criteria.description: Removed in WCAG 2.2. Previously required valid HTML parsing. Now considered obsolete as modern browsers handle parsing errors consistently.

success_criteria.name: Name, Role, Value
success_criteria.description: For all user interface components, the name, role, and values can be programmatically determined and set.

success_criteria.name: Status Messages
success_criteria.description: Status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties so they can be presented without receiving focus.
